request.question: Which Hesitation Wounds band member toured with Bruce Springsteen's band in 2009?

title: Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes
Rosalita (Come Out Tonight)
Jay Weinberg
Ed Manion
Frank Stefanko
The Fever (Bruce Springsteen song)
Hesitation Wounds
Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09
Hesitation Marks
Hesitation Wounds (album)
sentences: Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes are a Jersey Shore musical group led by Southside Johnny.
They have been recording albums since 1976 and are closely associated with Bruce Springsteen & the E Street Band.
They have recorded and/or performed several Springsteen songs, including "The Fever" and "Fade Away".
Springsteen has also performed with the band on numerous occasions and in 1991 guested on their "Better Days" album.

During the band's formative years Miami Steve Van Zandt acted as the band's co-leader, guitarist, songwriter, arranger and producer while other E Streeters including Clarence Clemons, Max Weinberg, Garry Tallent, Ernest Carter, Patti Scialfa and Soozie Tyrell have all performed, toured and/or recorded with the Jukes.
The band's horn section – the Miami Horns – has also toured and recorded with Springsteen.
More than one hundred musicians can claim to have been members of the Asbury Jukes, including Jon Bon Jovi who toured with the band as a special guest during 1990.
Bon Jovi has also cited the band as an influence and Jukes' Bobby Bandiera and Jeff Kazee have also toured with Bon Jovi.

Other notable band members include Mark Pender and Richie "La Bamba" Rosenberg who have played regularly with the Max Weinberg 7 on both "Late Night with Conan O'Brien" and "The Tonight Show with Conan O'Brien".

"Rosalita (Come Out Tonight)" is a 1973 song by Bruce Springsteen, from his "The Wild, the Innocent & the E Street Shuffle" album, and is especially famed as a concert number for Springsteen and The E Street Band.
The song, which clocks in at just over seven minutes, is a story of forbidden love between the singer and the titular Rosalita, whose parents disapprove of his life in a rock and roll band.
It is included on the compilation albums "The Essential Bruce Springsteen" and "Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band Greatest Hits".
Jay Weinberg (born September 8, 1990), is an American musician and drummer.
He is the son of American drummer Max Weinberg.
He has played with the American punk band the Reveling and toured in 2009 as a drummer with Bruce Springsteen's E Street Band, substituting for his father.
During 2010, he was briefly the drummer for Madball.
During 2011 and 2012, Weinberg played with Against Me!
In 2014, Weinberg became the drummer for the American heavy metal music band Slipknot.

Ed Manion (born February 28, 1952), also known as Eddie "Kingfish" Manion, is an American saxophonist, who plays both tenor and baritone sax.As a solo artist, he released his own instrumental album titled Nightlife in 2015.
Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.

He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band and also a member of Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour, later called Bruce Springsteen with The Sessions Band.
He is an original member of Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, The Miami Horns, and Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.

As a session musician, he has recorded, toured, and/or performed with, among others, Bruce Springsteen and The E Street Band, Diana Ross, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bon Jovi, Willy DeVille, Dave Edmunds, Bob Dylan, Keith Richards, Darlene Love, Ronnie Spector, Dion, The Allman Brothers Band, Kim Wilson, and Graham Parker.
As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.
Frank Stefanko is a fine art photographer with connections to New Jersey performers Patti Smith and Bruce Springsteen.
Stefanko's early photographs, taken in the 1960s through the 1980s, reveal the emerging careers of the two young artists.
Frank retains an on-going working relationship with both Springsteen and Smith.
Stefanko will be releasing a book in November of 2017, entitled "Bruce Springsteen: Further Up the Road" that chronicles the 40-year working relationship between himself and Bruce Springsteen.

"The Fever" is an early Bruce Springsteen song, recorded and released on an extremely rare 7" demo to promote the band, coupled with 'Rendezvous', a live track recorded in New York, although the venue and date remain a mystery - Rendezvous opens with Bruce calling out "New York!
Go ahead, Max!"
The single was released under The Jersey Devil and was pressed on Bruce Records, which was Springsteen's own independent label for his band.
The Fever itself was performed by Springsteen in concert beginning in March 1973.

It was recorded in 914 Sound Studios in May 1973, during sessions for Springsteen's second album "The Wild, the Innocent & the E Street Shuffle", but was only used as a demo by manager Mike Appel's Laurel Canyon publishing arm and renamed "Fever For the Girl".
In early 1974 Appel sent it to several progressive rock radio stations, where it became a "turntable hit" in the ramp-up of Springsteen expectations prior to "Born to Run".

The original Springsteen recording finally achieved an official release surfacing in 1999 on his "18 Tracks" outtakes collection- the live b-side has never been officially released since in any other format, although a different live version is on 18 Tracks and Tracks (Bruce Springsteen album) and a studio cut of Rendezvous was released on 'The Promise' CD in 2010.
Hesitation Wounds is an American hardcore punk supergroup formed in 2012.

The band features vocalist Jeremy Bolm of Touché Amoré; guitarist Neeraj Kane of The Hope Conspiracy and formerly of The Suicide File and Holy Fever; bassist Stephen LaCour of True Cross and formerly of Trap Them; and Jay Weinberg, the son of Max Weinberg (E Street Band, The Max Weinberg 7), who previously performed with Madball and Against Me!
, and eventually joined Slipknot.
Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09 is a live album by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, released on July 13, 2017.
It is the fourteenth such release by the Bruce Springsteen Archives.

The performance was Springsteen's final show at the soon to be demolished Wachovia Spectrum in Philadelphia, PA and featured a full album performance of "Born in the U.S.A.", the show opener, "The Price You Pay", which was performed for the first time since 1981 and guest appearances by former band member Vini Lopez and even Springsteen's mother Adele.
Hesitation Marks is the eighth studio album by American industrial rock band Nine Inch Nails, released on August 30, 2013 by Columbia Records.
It is the band's first release through Columbia, and their first album released on a major record label since "Year Zero" (2007).
The album's title is derived from the term "hesitation wounds", which is used to describe marks that are produced by testing a bladed weapon before attempting suicide or self-harming.
Hesitation Wounds is the third studio album by American hip hop artist Sleep, a member of the Pacific Northwest hip hop collective Oldominion.
It was released on Strange Famous Records on June 29, 2009.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise summary string (no extra fields, labels, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification (see below).
- Primary goal: integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the explicit, supported linkage it needs.

When the question requires linking the context entity to information in the passages and the passages supply the bridging fact:
- Produce a concise (1–2 sentence) integrated summary that (1) names the context entity (using the form present in `context`), (2) names the supporting entity/attribute found in `passages` (using the form present in the inputs), and (3) states the explicit relation or fact (membership, creator attribution, partnership, founding/establishment, award, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Use only facts present in `context` and `passages` and avoid introducing any unsupported details.

Name and attribution formatting:
- If a canonical multi-token name (given + family name or full official name) appears in the inputs, use that full form in the summary; do not truncate to a surname or partial name.
- For questions about creator/author/credit, prefer the person-name attribution as given in the inputs over returning the title of a work or event.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (for example, asks "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages. If multiple valid names are requested, return them comma-separated. Do not add explanatory clauses, relational fragments, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (e.g., "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Retain uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 sentences). Do not expand into long biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Do not assert facts that are not present in `context` and `passages`.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else:
   - For a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no.
   - For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces). Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if no multi-token form appears in either summary.

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

response.hop1_query: Which Hesitation Wounds band member toured with Bruce Springsteen's band in 2009?
hop1_docs: Working on a Dream Tour | The Working on a Dream Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, which began in April 2009 and ended in November 2009.  It followed the late January 2009 release of the album "Working on a Dream".  This was the first full E Street Band tour without founding member Danny Federici, who died during the previous tour in 2008, and the final tour for founding member Clarence Clemons, who died in 2011.

Ed Manion | Ed Manion (born February 28, 1952), also known as Eddie "Kingfish" Manion, is an American saxophonist, who plays both tenor and baritone sax.As a solo artist, he released his own instrumental album titled Nightlife in 2015.  Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band and also a member of Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour, later called Bruce Springsteen with The Sessions Band.  He is an original member of Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, The Miami Horns, and Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  As a session musician, he has recorded, toured, and/or performed with, among others, Bruce Springsteen and The E Street Band, Diana Ross, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bon Jovi, Willy DeVille, Dave Edmunds, Bob Dylan, Keith Richards, Darlene Love, Ronnie Spector, Dion, The Allman Brothers Band, Kim Wilson, and Graham Parker.  As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.

Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour | The Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour was a concert tour featuring Bruce Springsteen and a new backing band, that took place from mid-1992 to mid-1993.  It followed the simultaneous release of his albums "Human Touch" and "Lucky Town" earlier in 1992.  It was his first of four non-E Street Band tours.  Later, Springsteen had more non-E Street Band tours, the Ghost of Tom Joad Tour, the Seeger Sessions Tour, and the Devils & Dust Tour.  The tour was not as commercially or critically successful as past tours, due to poor reception of Human Touch and Lucky Town as well as changes from previous tours.  According to Springsteen biographer Dave Marsh, die-hard fans have informally referred to the backing band as "the Other Band" (and the tour as "The Other Band Tour").
Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour | Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour
Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour | Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour

Wrecking Ball World Tour | The Wrecking Ball World Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band to promote Springsteen's seventeenth studio album, "Wrecking Ball", which was released on March 5, 2012.  It was the first tour for the E Street Band without founding member Clarence Clemons, who died on June 18, 2011.  The world-wide tour in support of the album, which ended in September 2013, reached 26 countries, the most ever for one of Springsteen's tours.  The tour was resumed in January 2014 however this time it was to promote his new album, "High Hopes" and went under that album's name.
Magic Tour (Bruce Springsteen) | The Magic Tour was Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band's 2007–2008 concert tour of North America and Western Europe.
hop1_titles: Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour
Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour
response.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- "Working on a Dream Tour" — [1] quote: "began in April 2009 and ended in November 2009."
- "Ed Manion" — [2] quote: "He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band"
- "Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour" — [2] quote: "Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour"

Ambiguities:
- "Hesitation Wounds" — not mentioned in any passage (no passage labels); membership unknown from these passages.
- Possible candidate overlap: "Ed Manion" is a documented Springsteen touring musician here, but passages do not state any connection between Ed Manion and Hesitation Wounds.

Bridge / missing fact:
- Which specific Hesitation Wounds band member toured with Bruce Springsteen in 2009 (name of member) is not present.
- Confirmation that that member toured in 2009 (year and tour name) and that they are/ were a Hesitation Wounds member is missing.

Retrieval direction:
- Fetch the band page: "Hesitation Wounds" (Wikipedia page titled exactly "Hesitation Wounds").
- Search query: "Hesitation Wounds band members" (to list member names and verify membership).
- Search query: "Hesitation Wounds [member name] Bruce Springsteen 2009" — run this for any member found (e.g., "Hesitation Wounds Jeremy Bolm Bruce Springsteen 2009").
- Search query: "Which Hesitation Wounds member toured with Bruce Springsteen" or "Hesitation Wounds toured with Bruce Springsteen 2009".
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.
hop2_docs: Hesitation Wounds (album) | Hesitation Wounds is the third studio album by American hip hop artist Sleep, a member of the Pacific Northwest hip hop collective Oldominion.  It was released on Strange Famous Records on June 29, 2009.

Ed Manion | Ed Manion (born February 28, 1952), also known as Eddie "Kingfish" Manion, is an American saxophonist, who plays both tenor and baritone sax.As a solo artist, he released his own instrumental album titled Nightlife in 2015.  Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band and also a member of Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour, later called Bruce Springsteen with The Sessions Band.  He is an original member of Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, The Miami Horns, and Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  As a session musician, he has recorded, toured, and/or performed with, among others, Bruce Springsteen and The E Street Band, Diana Ross, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bon Jovi, Willy DeVille, Dave Edmunds, Bob Dylan, Keith Richards, Darlene Love, Ronnie Spector, Dion, The Allman Brothers Band, Kim Wilson, and Graham Parker.  As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.

The Essential Bruce Springsteen | The Essential Bruce Springsteen is a compilation album by Bruce Springsteen, released November 11, 2003.  The collection is part of a series of "Essential" sets released by Sony BMG (previously Sony Music Entertainment).  It includes songs from various Springsteen albums and concerts up to the year 2003.

Working on a Dream Tour | The Working on a Dream Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, which began in April 2009 and ended in November 2009.  It followed the late January 2009 release of the album "Working on a Dream".  This was the first full E Street Band tour without founding member Danny Federici, who died during the previous tour in 2008, and the final tour for founding member Clarence Clemons, who died in 2011.

Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09 | Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09 is a live album by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, released on July 13, 2017.  It is the fourteenth such release by the Bruce Springsteen Archives.  The performance was Springsteen's final show at the soon to be demolished Wachovia Spectrum in Philadelphia, PA and featured a full album performance of "Born in the U.S.A.", the show opener, "The Price You Pay", which was performed for the first time since 1981 and guest appearances by former band member Vini Lopez and even Springsteen's mother Adele.

Dear Bruce Springsteen | Dear Bruce Springsteen is a juvenile fiction novel published in 1987 by Kevin Major.  It consists of the letters of 14-year-old Terry Blanchard to his idol, Bruce Springsteen, in which he talks about his problems as a youth.

The River (Bruce Springsteen album) | The River is the fifth studio album by Bruce Springsteen.  It was released on October 17, 1980, by Columbia Records.  Springsteen's only double album, "The River" was produced by Jon Landau, Springsteen and bandmate Steven Van Zandt.  The album was Springsteen's first to go #1 on the "Billboard 200" and spent four weeks at the top of chart.
response.summary_2: No supporting evidence in the provided passages.
